resource_contract.detail: A fixed corpus proves typed detector and observable limits, target construction, value semantics, canonical text, standalone parsing, and the explicit numeric shift-target boundary.
Fixed-size instructions prove every selected kind, constructor, accessor, value contract, canonical rendering, tag, probability, coordinate, target-role, separator, and exact-target-count rule.
Fixed-size empty, single, and separator-delimited target lists prove grouping semantics without a resource claim.

A fixed corpus proves comments, case normalization, whitespace, CRLF input, tags, escaped tags, repeats, every instruction kind, canonical printing, parse-print-parse equality, and representative malformed syntax.

A bounded model proves default and explicit construction, value semantics, instruction and zero/nonzero DEM-repeat insertion, item access and ranges, atomic text append, clear, repeat accessors, canonical rendering, and the distinction between DEM and circuit repeat-count domains.
A compact repeated model proves detector shift, detector count, observable count, error count, coordinate shift, and checked detector-shift overflow without flattening.
A fixed repeated model proves all-detector, selected-detector, and single-detector coordinate queries plus shifted coordinate accumulation and out-of-range rejection.
The generated distance-five, seven-round surface-code case proves folded DEM coordinates terminate and exactly match independently queried circuit coordinates.

A small repeated model proves adjusted detector and coordinate shifts for lazy and materialized flattening; a trillion-repeat model proves bounded lazy prefixes while materialized expansion fails closed.
A compact nested model proves probability-only rounding, zero retention, recursive tag removal, repeat preservation, and source immutability.
One exact structural selector binds the imported basic, repeat, and selected Rust model API rows to the independently owned model value and mutation contract.
The exact structural selector binds the imported generated surface-code coordinate row while the independent canonical parent owns the upstream nontermination regression.
One exact aggregate selector binds the imported C++ and Python DEM-instruction fixture rows to the independently owned target, validation, print, and grouping contracts.
The exact imported PF1 selector proves repeated error counting plus all, selected, and single-detector coordinate queries with empty defaults and out-of-range rejection.
One exact aggregate selector binds the imported transform row to independent flattening, rounding, zero-retention, tag-removal, repeat-preservation, and expansion-limit contracts.
One exact aggregate selector binds the imported validation row to typed-id, target, instruction, parser, repeat, tag, and non-finite-coordinate rejection contracts.
The exact imported resource selector proves a full coordinate map rejects more than one million materialized detectors while selected and single-detector queries fold through the same repeat.
This case proves canonical parse-print-parse behavior; parser admission and resource limits have independent CQ owners.

A fixed corpus proves accepted whitespace, aliases, comments, fusion, repeats, record and Pauli targets plus malformed gate, argument, target, and record forms; parser-wide admission limits have independent CQ owners.

This case proves canonical printing of aliases, plain and classical pair targets, measurement targets, detector and observable records, probabilities, Pauli lists, and empty, tagged-empty, and nested-empty repeats.
Fixed-size instructions prove sweep controls are accepted in the controlled position and rejected by single-qubit gates.
This case proves simple, empty, escaped, and canonical tags plus preservation of nested repeat-block structure without a resource claim.
This case proves MPP product grouping and optional measurement probability canonicalization without a resource claim.
This case proves SPP and SPP_DAG Pauli-product parsing and canonical printing without a resource claim.
This case proves successful typed circuit file input and canonical output; path errors and size admission have separate cases.
This case proves canonical text append behavior across tags, repeats, and fusion boundaries without a resource claim.
This case proves the public append_from_stim_program_text alias delegates to canonical append semantics.
This case proves construction and extraction boundaries for typed negative measurement-record offsets.
This case proves accepted and rejected qubit, record, sweep, and Pauli target text boundaries.
This case proves typed extraction from qubit, Pauli, record, sweep, and combiner targets.
This case proves invertible result targets and rejection of combiner, record, and sweep inversion.
This case proves classical, Pauli, qubit, result, and combiner target classification.
Fixed-size malformed repeat grammar is rejected without mutating an existing circuit, including zero counts, missing counts, extra braces, and non-repeat braces.
Fixed-size typed argument and target vectors prove pair cardinality, duplicate-target, Pauli-role, inversion, and probability validation before instruction construction.
Fixed-size one- and two-qubit Pauli-channel inputs prove exact list arity, closed-unit entries, disjoint sums, pair cardinality, and typed NaN rejection.
This case proves recursive tag removal while preserving repeat structure and instruction arguments.
Fixed-size circuits prove canonical fusion, non-fusion, invalid append rejection, and fusion across a concatenation boundary.
Each fixed-size syntax record is parsed into a bounded materialized circuit or rejected before a circuit is returned; parser-wide size and nesting limits have independent CQ owners.
The fixed-size input remains compact while proving large-repeat parsing, finite signed coordinates, exponent rejection, and CRLF equivalence without expanding the repeat.
This case proves empty, singleton, pair, Pauli-product, measurement-pad, and coordinate target grouping and constructs every non-REPEAT gate shape.
A fixed-size typed instruction proves canonical printing and eager pair-target cardinality validation.

The first-invalid pair-target case rejects while constructing a single-target instruction; target grouping and segmentation materialize output only for the fixed small instruction corpus, without an asymptotic allocation claim.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: fixed CircuitInstruction constructor, tag-normalization, accessor, complete upstream target-group matrix, segmented-preservation, clone, equality, and rejection variants

resource_contract.detail: This fixed corpus proves zero, pair-measurement, per-target measurement, and measurement-pad count semantics.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: fixed instruction measurement-count gate and target-shape variants

resource_contract.detail: Fixed boundary values prove typed qubit, detector, observable, and repeat-count construction without materializing circuit state.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: zero, maximum valid, and first-invalid typed circuit identifiers

resource_contract.detail: The first-invalid zero repeat rejects before a block is constructed; the fixed value corpus clones only selected small circuit and repeat bodies and proves body, count, tag, and mutation independence.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: empty, instruction, tagged instruction, nested target and argument inequality, repeat-block body/count/tag inequality, zero-count rejection, clone, and mutation-independence variants

resource_contract.detail: This fixed semantic corpus proves instruction and repeat-block append ownership, tag preservation, adjacent fusion, and required non-fusion cases without making a separate resource claim.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: tagged fusing instructions and one tagged repeat block

resource_contract.detail: This fixed semantic corpus proves independent forward and reverse iterator clones over nested repeats without making a separate traversal-memory claim.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: nested repeat forward and reverse iterator clone positions

resource_contract.detail: The finite public error-variant corpus proves exhaustive typed equality, exact display text, and CircuitResult error propagation.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: all public CircuitError variants plus typed CircuitResult error propagation

resource_contract.detail: Scalar count results use parsed-circuit nesting state instead of materializing trillion-count logical repetitions, and checked arithmetic rejects the first overflowing folded count.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: empty construction, append mutation, result-group counts, trillion-count folded repeats, and checked overflow

resource_contract.detail: Qubit width returns one scalar while traversing nested 999,999-count repeats without materializing logical iterations.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: empty, plain, annotation, measurement-pad, and five-level huge-repeat qubit-width cases

resource_contract.detail: Nested billion-scale coordinate shifts use parsed-circuit nesting state instead of materializing logical repetitions and return one bounded coordinate vector.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: three nested coordinate-shift repeat dimensions

resource_contract.detail: This fixed semantic case proves final coordinates while a separate qualification case owns huge-repeat folding.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: qubit coordinates before, within, and after repeated shifts

resource_contract.detail: Four nested repeat counts represent 64 trillion logical inner iterations while coordinate evaluation uses parsed-circuit nesting state plus the ten addressed-qubit results.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: pinned Stim four-level huge-repeat coordinate circuit

resource_contract.detail: This fixed semantic case proves the empty post-clear invariant without a separate allocation claim.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: instruction, measurement, and detector state cleared to empty

resource_contract.detail: Zero and one repetition use empty or cloned circuits, larger counts use one folded repeat block, and multiplication that overflows the repeat count rejects before expansion.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: zero, one, folded two and three, nested fusion, 1.23e18 count, and overflow cases

resource_contract.detail: This fixed semantic case proves recursive tag removal and source independence without a separate allocation limit claim.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: top-level, repeat, measurement, detector, record-target, and source-independence tag cases

resource_contract.detail: The aggregate proves the one-million-instruction materialization admission boundary, folded shift-only huge repeats, and coordinate-overflow rejection in addition to exact flattened semantics.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: basic, coordinate, tagged, flattened-operation, huge-repeat, cap-rejection, and overflow cases

resource_contract.detail: This fixed semantic aggregate proves ordinary and identity-error removal, measurement-noise stripping, heralded-result replacement, tag preservation, and annotation preservation without a separate size claim.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: ordinary, measurement, heralded, identity-error, tagged, annotation, and record-preservation cases

resource_contract.detail: This fixed semantic aggregate proves the selected ISWAP, MPP, SPP, pair-measurement, noise, annotation, tag, constant-product, and flow-preservation decomposition scope.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: public example, tagged-noise, constant-product, anti-Hermitian, and measurement-rich flow cases

resource_contract.detail: This fixed selected-scope aggregate proves single- and two-qubit Clifford simplification, recursive repeats, unsupported-gate preservation, and classical-control preservation.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: single-qubit, two-qubit, repeated, unsupported, and classical-control simplification cases

resource_contract.detail: This fixed selected-scope aggregate proves unsupported-category None results and canonical measurement, reset, Pauli, phase, identity, and CX decomposition text.

static_property_plan.generator_domain: nine unsupported representatives plus exact canonical output and reparsing for all seventeen implemented measurement, reset, Pauli, phase, identity, and CX gates

resource_contract.detail: Exact fixed records prove 01, b8, hits, dets, and r8 writer bytes plus writer construction, value semantics, result-type transitions, and extraction.
Exact records prove empty and repeated dets boundaries, long r8 gaps, and non-byte-aligned bit-slice writes.
A five-shot table proves shot-major batch writing in every selected non-PTB64 format and the exact 64-shot PTB64 measurement-major word layout.
A 100-bit reference-sample table proves exact sparse, packed, run-length, typed dets, and PTB64 bytes including one shot-local flip.
The exact 102-measurement sequence proves append, lookback, unwritten output, compaction, storage reporting, cloning, equality, and debug behavior.
Fixed scalar and batch histories prove zero, available, beginning-of-time, and configured maximum lookback boundaries.
The pinned 1102-measurement five-shot sequence proves intermediate 256-row flushing, final output, written cursors, lookback retention, and compaction.
A fixed five-shot history proves editable zero rows, width rejection, lookback values, cloning, equality, and debug behavior.
A 300-measurement regression proves complete chunks flush once, reference bits use global measurement indices, retained storage stays bounded, and final output appends only the remainder.
A two-shot fixed record proves a short reference sample is measurement-indexed and zero-padded instead of being interpreted as one reference bit per shot.
Pinned 18-bit records prove materialized, dense streaming, packed streaming, and sparse streaming decoding for 01, b8, hits, and r8.
Deterministic 64, 128, 256, and 504-bit multi-record corpora prove all selected non-PTB64 formats through materialized, dense, packed, and sparse APIs.
Fixed layouts and tokens prove checked M/D/L width construction, measurement-only construction, namespace counts, total width, prefixes, token values, and width-overflow rejection.

The checked pinned-Stim corpus proves LF and CRLF handling, DETS EOF handling, empty HITS and DETS records, typed namespace offsets, duplicate semantics, and reusable materialized, dense, packed, sparse, token, and SparseShot consumers.
A circuit-derived detector and observable layout proves typed D/L offsets and every 01, b8, r8, hits, and dets conversion direction.
Typed dets input proves detector and observable namespaces stay distinct through observable index 1000 and flatten into the declared layout exactly.

The checked pinned-Stim corpus rejects unterminated 01 and HITS records, malformed separators, invalid bytes, numeric overflow, namespace crossings, out-of-range tokens, and malformed DETS prefixes through every applicable reader family.
Repeated HITS tokens prove dense materialized, dense streaming, and packed streaming readers retain Stim's parity semantics.
Repeated and out-of-order sparse tokens prove sparse visitors preserve the source token sequence without sorting or deduplication.
Two 64-shot groups of 71-bit records prove PTB64 layout, inferred counts, bounded prefixes, dense and sparse reconstruction, and exact group, width, and truncation failures.
A deterministic 576 by 1000-bit table proves exact write/read round trips across all selected formats, including nine complete PTB64 groups.
Every public streaming visitor family stops after its first callback error without parsing or visiting a later record.
Fixed sparse shots prove public fields, construction, cloning, equality, debug output, Stim-style text, and low-64 observable mask semantics.
Circuit-derived measurement layout converts exact 01, b8, hits, and r8 records to typed dets output.
Circuit-derived detector and observable records prove primary typed dets output and exact observable side-output routing.
Explicit measurement, detector, and observable counts plus DEM-derived layouts prove every selected non-PTB64 conversion direction.
Measurement layouts and raw bits-per-shot mode prove every selected non-PTB64 format plus an exact 2048-bit b8 record.
Exact CLI failures prove circuit types are mandatory and unique, layout information is required, and raw bits cannot be emitted as typed dets.
Typed CLI DETS conversion sets repeated dense tokens, matching Stim's dense DETS semantics without applying HITS parity.
The checked 62-case corpus proves exact convert output plus matching acceptance for every applicable m2d and sample_dem replay path without independent CLI grammar normalization.
sample_dem HITS replay consumes duplicate sparse indices as set membership, matching pinned Stim's table-minor replay reader while ordinary dense HITS readers retain their separate parity contract.
convert writes each completed record before parsing the next record, preserving pinned Stim's successful stdout prefix when a later input record is malformed.
The shared m2d and replay text-line reader rejects an over-limit available chunk before consuming it or extending its retained line buffer past the source-owned cap.
The retained output owner truncates regular files but writes special files such as /dev/null as streams without set_len or seek, including mixed regular and special multi-output commands.
The Stab-specific stim-to-stim extension validates mode-specific options before preflight, rejects inactive circuit, DEM, layout, and observable roles, and leaves their paths untouched.
All explicit input/output and output/output role pairs for sample, detect, m2d, analyze_errors, sample_dem, and convert reject direct aliases before any retained sentinel is truncated.
The complete file-role matrix rejects lexically distinct normalized-relative aliases before truncation.
The complete file-role matrix rejects output symlinks that resolve to retained inputs or other outputs before truncation.
The complete file-role matrix rejects distinct directory entries that identify the same hard-linked file before truncation.
Zero-shot sample, detect, and sample_dem still execute role preflight before their empty-output shortcut.
Input/input identity remains legal: m2d can retain independent measurement and sweep handles for the same file without weakening input/output rejection.
This exact structural selector binds the imported writer coverage row to independently owned exact format, boundary, bit-slice, and table contracts.
This exact structural selector binds the imported reader coverage row to independently owned exact format, width, malformed-input, typed-layout, and PTB64 contracts.
This exact structural selector binds the imported scalar measurement-record row to the independently owned state, lookback, output, and compaction contracts.
This exact structural selector binds the imported batch-record row to independently owned chunking, reference, lookback, editable-zero, and compaction contracts.
This exact structural selector binds the imported batch-writer row to the independently owned shot-major and PTB64 table contracts.
This exact structural selector binds the imported sparse-shot row to the independently owned value, text, field, and observable-mask contracts.
This exact structural selector binds the imported convert coverage row to independently owned circuit, DEM, explicit-layout, raw-width, PTB64, side-output, and failure contracts.
The frozen 81-gate registry and alias corpus is fixed-size; lookup traverses bounded static metadata without input-sized allocation.
Gate-name lookup accepts only bounded gate identifiers and resolves through the static registry without caller-controlled allocation growth.
The exact Stim v1.16.0 hash corpus contains 82 fixed names and expected values, including NOT_A_GATE, and does not grow with caller input.
Unitary metadata is restricted to fixed 2x2 and 4x4 matrices, so conversion and inverse checks use constant bounded memory.
Each gate owns one bounded static H/S/CX/M/R decomposition string; parsing materializes only that fixed decomposition.
A gate target is one bounded typed value; parsing rejects overlong or out-of-range identifiers before allocating domain state.
Target constructors and accessors operate on a single bounded enum value and allocate no input-sized storage.
Target inversion transforms one bounded value in constant space and rejects non-result target kinds immediately.
Target classification is a constant-time enum inspection with no allocation.
Owned storage is exactly ceil(width/64) words and borrowed views allocate nothing; the deterministic corpus spans empty, unaligned, word, SIMD-lane, multi-lane, and 65,537-bit widths.
Each in-place logical operation traverses the preallocated ceil(width/64) words with constant SIMD scratch and canonicalizes the logical tail without allocating output storage.
Copy and range-XOR mutate existing destination storage, validate both checked ranges before mutation, use constant word scratch, and clear padding beyond the logical width.
A BitBlock is exactly four u64 lanes; constructors, accessors, Boolean kernels, and popcount use fixed-size values with no input-sized allocation.
Construction checks rows times words-per-row before allocation; row access and mutation stay within the preallocated matrix, and row kernels use constant SIMD scratch.

Out-of-place transpose allocates exactly one checked transposed result buffer without mutating its source; in-place square transpose uses bounded stack scratch with zero heap allocation and rejects rectangular shapes before mutation.

Sparse construction and XOR allocate at most the combined input cardinality; the deterministic corpus crosses stack and heap merge thresholds and compares every result with a dense bit-vector reference.
Each integer helper performs one bounded machine-word calculation with no allocation; the qualification corpus exhausts 0 through 65,536 and checks upper-word boundaries.
The typed distance and round constructors validate scalar domains without allocation; task values and immutable noise builders operate on fixed-size parameter values.

The exact noisy distance-four case proves layout, hint, folded 5,000-round circuit bytes, generated-value semantics, and accessors; the independent resource case owns the maximum distance and round boundaries.

The complete pinned cases prove exact noisy unrotated Z and one-round X circuits, exact noisy rotated distance-two bytes, and exact rotated distance-four and distance-five layouts; the independent resource case owns quadratic admission.
The complete pinned case proves the exact distance-five layout plus exact noisy distance-three circuit, layout, and hint; the independent resource case owns quadratic admission.
Round and parity checks reject before color-code coordinate allocation, while the minimum valid two-round distance-three circuit is materialized as the acceptance boundary.

The complete seven-distance by seven-round task matrix materializes circuits only through distance fifteen, checks exact detector counts, one observable, and absence of noise instructions, and deterministically analyzes every circuit into an error-free detector error model. One semantic sample per task, one 256-shot portable batch, and the exact Python five-shot dimensions retain execution-path and multiword evidence without claiming C++ SIMD word-size identity.

Generation preflights a 131,072 physical-qubit limit before allocating quadratic coordinate maps, target vectors, circuits, layouts, or CLI text. Repetition accepts the nominal domain maximum 2047 and the typed constructor rejects 2048; the nearest family-valid accepted and first resource-rejected distances are rotated surface 256/257, unrotated surface 181/182, and color 341/343. Accepted materialized coordinate cardinalities must equal their projections, rejected calls have constant allocation bounds, and maximum u64 rounds remain folded.
The public gen command propagates rotated, unrotated, and color projection failures with empty stdout; an oversized color request with --out rejects before the output path is created.
